No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the syllabus links to poverty eradication. The course focuses on cultural industries and general competencies.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the syllabus links to hunger or food security. The course content does not touch upon these areas.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the syllabus links to health or well-being. The course content is unrelated to this SDG.

Quality Education.reason: Core Competency B ('identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues') and the course's 'Cultural and Creative Industries' focus strongly align with Target 4.7 (education for sustainable development, cultural diversity).
evidence: Cultural and Creative Industries Case Studies
Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the syllabus links to gender equality. While 'social responsibility' is broad, it doesn't explicitly cover this SDG.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the syllabus links to clean water and sanitation. The course content is unrelated to this SDG.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the syllabus links to affordable and clean energy. The course content is unrelated to this SDG.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The 'Cultural and Creative Industries' focus promotes creativity, innovation, and entrepreneurship (Target 8.3). 'Social responsibility' (Core Competency B) also supports decent work.
evidence: Cultural and Creative Industries Case Studies

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The 'Cultural and Creative Industries' are key for innovation and sustainable industrialization (Targets 9.2, 9.5). The 'sustainability issues' competency further strengthens this link (Target 9.4).
evidence: Cultural and Creative Industries Case Studies

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the syllabus links to reducing inequalities. 'Social responsibility' is too general to specifically imply this SDG.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course's focus on 'Cultural and Creative Industries' has a strong inferred link to safeguarding cultural heritage (Target 11.4), a key component of sustainable communities.
evidence: Cultural and Creative Industries Case Studies

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competency B, 'understand and identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' directly supports adopting sustainable corporate practices (Target 12.6).
evidence: Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.

Climate Action.reason: No direct or strong indirect evidence in the syllabus links to climate action. 'Sustainability issues' is too general to specifically imply climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the syllabus links to marine life or oceans. The course content is unrelated to this SDG.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity. The course content is unrelated to this SDG.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The explicit mention of 'Respect Copyright' (維護智慧財產權) relates to the rule of law (Target 16.3), and 'ethics' (Core Competency B) supports strong institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The 'teamwork' core competency is foundational for partnerships, but the syllabus lacks explicit or strong implicit links to global partnerships for SDG achievement.
evidence: 團隊合作與領導能力(Students will be able to work effectively in teams as well as develop leadership skills.)
